latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: ASET – Academic Safety and Evidencing Truth
latest.description: ASET (Academic Safety and Evidencing Truth) is an AI-powered claim verification platform built on AWS that verifies scientific claims against 1.2M+ peer-reviewed papers across 8 domains — and automatically fetches and permanently stores new papers from arXiv, PubMed, etc. when gaps are found, making the knowledge base self-growing with every query. From typed claims to YouTube videos and uploaded documents, ASET delivers evidence-backed trust scores in under 10 seconds.

latest.title: From Zero to Deployed☁️: Securing AWS with IAM, EC2, and Nginx on Ubuntu
latest.description:
As part of the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp(Week 3), 2026, I recently completed a hands-on project focused on a critical cloud reality: building is easy, but building securely and efficiently requires intention.
My mission was to deploy a live web server while maintaining a strict security posture and total cost visibility. Here’s how I built a secure, cost-aware environment using IAM, EC2, and Billing tools.

latest.title: Hands-On Lab: Scaling Infrastructure with EC2, IAM, and Security
latest.description: In this technical lab, I moved beyond basic instance launching to implement a secure, monitored web environment. This project focused on the intersection of Compute (EC2), Identity and Access Management (IAM), and Operational Excellence through billing alerts

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: Afrifashion 3D - From Fashion Images to Sewing Patterns with AI
latest.description: Afrifashion 3D is an AI-powered platform that analyzes garment images to identify clothing components, generate sewing pattern diagrams, and produce explainer videos that guide garment production. By combining generative AI with a scalable cloud architecture on AWS, the system helps designers and fashion businesses accelerate the process of turning garment ideas into manufacturable designs.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: TruthLayer — The Five-Signal AI Verification Engine
latest.description: TruthLayer is a production-ready AI verification engine that intercepts hallucinations before they reach users. Five independent signals. Platt-calibrated confidence. Proven via McNemar's test (p < 0.001). 95.33% precision on 300 adversarial cases. 873ms. $1.50/month Zero external ML dependencies. One API call. No model changes required.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: Vibe Matching — Fix the First 5 Minutes of Human Connection
latest.description:
AI digital twins talk in advance to reduce uncertainty and psychological friction in first meetings. 
Through validation, this emerged not as a compatibility predictor, but as a "Conversation Catalyst" that helps people take the first step.

latest.title: Hands-On AWS: From Cost Control to Compute.
latest.description: An article of an educational hands-on project, covering everything from Billing alarms to deploying on EC2 instances.

latest.title: A Guide to Deploying Secure EC2 Web Servers Using IAM Guardrails
latest.description: Follow my journey through the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp as I transition from basic cloud concepts to managing secure, production ready infrastructure. I share how I navigated AWS to build a resilient web server protected by custom IAM JSON policies and billing alerts.

latest.title: AI Context Window Overflow: Memory Pointer Fix
latest.description: AI agents fail silently when tool outputs exceed the context window. The Memory Pointer Pattern stores data externally and passes references.

latest.title: My AWS  Learning Journey ( EC2 ,IAM & Billing
latest.description: This covers the week3 journey of my aws cloud  bootcamp unilorin.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: VERA, A Multi-Agent Triage System for Healthcare in Chile
trending.description: A multi-agent healthcare triage system that screens patients in Chilean Spanish, detects crisis signals, and generates clinical reports. Built by two people on AWS for $17/month, designed to integrate with Chile's public health system via HL7 FHIR.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Ivy - The World's First Offline-Capable, Proactive AI Tutoring Agent
trending.description: A proactive AI tutoring agent powered by Amazon Bedrock that monitors student progress, initiates outbound voice calls, and runs on-device inference for offline learning addressing educational inequity for millions of Ethiopian students with intermittent connectivity.

trending.title: Cómo crear un agente de IA desde cero. Open source, local y gratis.
trending.description: De un modelo que solo responde a uno que busca, razona y recuerda. Todo con código que puedes leer, forkear y contribuir.

trending.title: Introducing Article View Counts on AWS Builder Center
trending.description: You can now see how many builders are reading your articles, giving you a real metric to understand the reach of your content.

trending.title: My manager asked if it could run itself. Here's how I automated iam-audit with Fargate, EventBridge and Terraform. Part 3
trending.description: From running a script manually every Monday to receiving it automatically at 9am — this post covers how I automated iam-audit using ECS Fargate, EventBridge Scheduler, and Terraform. No persistent infrastructure, no idle costs. Just an ephemeral task that spins up, audits 20+ AWS accounts, uploads the report to S3, sends a Slack notification with a presigned URL, and disappears. Includes design decisions, the partial backend pattern, and why secrets belong in Secrets Manager — not environment variables.

trending.title: Serverless RAG as a Service: A CDK Stack to Expose Your Bedrock Knowledge Base as an API and Connect It to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway
trending.description: Turn your Amazon Bedrock KB into a callable API and connect it to AgentCore GW as an MCP tool, all with AWS CDK. This post walks through a production-ready CDK stack deploying an API Gateway backed by Lambda, secured with SigV4, and designed to meet AC GW's requirements: explicit operationId fields, IAM outbound authorization, and an OpenAPI definition discoverable via GetExport. To expose your KB to apps or plug it into an AI agent, this pattern gives you a secure, repeatable, and extensible foundation.

trending.title: Breaking the Glass Ceiling of Low-Code: Migrating Automations to AWS
trending.description: Outgrowing low-code/no-code platforms? Learn how to migrate your workflows to AWS. We explore mapping webhooks and actions to API Gateway, Lambda, and Step Functions as an example.

trending.title: Building Secure Multi-Party Data Collaboration with AWS Clean Rooms
trending.description: Learn how to implement privacy-preserving analytics that enables organizations to collaborate on data insights without sharing raw data
